Quote reply
bchang0999
Oct 3, 2022
-
Hey guys im trying to deploy my project.
I run Vercel Build through my terminal it comes back with no errors but when run vercel deploy I keep getting
Build Failed Command "npm run build" exited with 1
Any suggestions or fixes would be greatly apreciated!
I see it says I need a projectId but my .env.local contains my id
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID=8a64h111
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000/```
I'm really stumped here.
```23:27:55.917 | Error: Configuration must contain `projectId`
23:27:55.917 | at exports.initConfig (/vercel/path0/node_modules/@sanity/client/lib/config.js:42:11)
23:27:55.918 | at SanityClient.config (/vercel/path0/node_modules/@sanity/client/lib/sanityClient.js:89:25)
23:27:55.918 | at new SanityClient (/vercel/path0/node_modules/@sanity/client/lib/sanityClient.js:53:8)
23:27:55.918 | at SanityClient (/vercel/path0/node_modules/@sanity/client/lib/sanityClient.js:50:12)
23:27:55.918 | at exports.createClient (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next-sanity/dist/next-sanity.cjs.production.min.js:1:1023)
23:27:55.918 | at Object.5149 (/vercel/path0/.next/server/pages/index.js:1055:75)
23:27:55.919 | at __webpack_require__ (/vercel/path0/.next/server/webpack-runtime.js:25:42)
23:27:55.919 | at /vercel/path0/.next/server/pages/index.js:20:65
23:27:55.919 | at Function.__webpack_require__.a (/vercel/path0/.next/server/webpack-runtime.js:89:13)
23:27:55.919 | at Object.2265 (/vercel/path0/.next/server/pages/index.js:11:21)
23:27:55.920 |
23:27:55.920 | > Build error occurred
23:27:55.922 | Error: Failed to collect page data for /
23:27:55.922 | at /vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/build/utils.js:916:15 {
23:27:55.922 | type: 'Error'
23:27:55.923 | }
23:27:55.978 | Error: Command "npm run build" exited with 1```
Beta
Was this translation helpful?

You must be logged in to vote
All reactions
Answered by
icyJoseph
Oct 3, 2022
Are you including your
.env.local
to the repository (you shouldn't anyway, so don't do it)? Or are you injecting it in the
Vercel
dashboard and doing a
vercel pull <environment>
kind of thing?
Edit:
I found your repo and you CORRECTLY skip including
.env.local
to the repository, but you have to do it in the
Vercel
dashboard.
Normally you need to go on the
Vercel
dashboard and define the env variables there, for preview and production environments.
I cloned your project and ran
npm run build
without env vars and I got the exact same error, so it must be that you haven't defined them in the
Vercel
dashboard.
I do believe that the
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
will probably give you trouble later, bu…
View full answer
Replies:
3 comments
·
11 replies

icyJoseph
Oct 3, 2022
Maintainer
-
Are you including your
.env.local
to the repository (you shouldn't anyway, so don't do it)? Or are you injecting it in the
Vercel
dashboard and doing a
vercel pull <environment>
kind of thing?
Edit:
I found your repo and you CORRECTLY skip including
.env.local
to the repository, but you have to do it in the
Vercel
dashboard.
Normally you need to go on the
Vercel
dashboard and define the env variables there, for preview and production environments.
I cloned your project and ran
npm run build
without env vars and I got the exact same error, so it must be that you haven't defined them in the
Vercel
dashboard.
I do believe that the
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
will probably give you trouble later, but we'll see.
Beta

Give feedback.
Marked as answer

bchang0999
Oct 3, 2022
Author
-
On vercel when adding env variables, the name I put was NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID (names couldnt have symbols in them) and then I added in value
abcdefgh (whatever the id is)
I did the same for NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID
NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
Was this the correct way to add it?
Also a new error occurs
Error occurred prerendering page "/". Read more:
https://nextjs.org/docs/messages/prerender-error
13:25:04.823 | FetchError: request to
http://localhost:3000/api/getPageInfo
failed, reason: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:3000
13:25:04.824 | at ClientRequest. (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/compiled/node-fetch/index.js:1:65756)
13:25:04.824 | at ClientRequest.emit (node:events:527:28)
13:25:04.825 | at Socket.socketErrorListener (node:_http_client:454:9)
13:25:04.825 | at Socket.emit (node:events:527:28)
13:25:04.825 | at emitErrorNT (node:internal/streams/destroy:157:8)
13:25:04.826 | at emitErrorCloseNT (node:internal/streams/destroy:122:3)
13:25:04.826 | at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:83:21)
13:25:04.827 |
13:25:04.827 | > Build error occurred
13:25:04.828 | info  - Generating static pages (3/3)
13:25:04.828 | Error: Export encountered errors on following paths:
13:25:04.829 | /
13:25:04.829 | at /vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/export/index.js:404:19
13:25:04.829 | at runMicrotasks ()
13:25:04.830 | at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:96:5)
13:25:04.830 | at async Span.traceAsyncFn (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/trace/trace.js:79:20)
13:25:04.830 | at async /vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/build/index.js:1229:21
13:25:04.831 | at async Span.traceAsyncFn (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/trace/trace.js:79:20)
13:25:04.831 | at async /vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/build/index.js:1091:17
13:25:04.832 | at async Span.traceAsyncFn (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/trace/trace.js:79:20)
13:25:04.832 | at async Object.build [as default] (/vercel/path0/node_modules/next/dist/build/index.js:65:29)
13:25:04.891 | Error: Command "npm run build" exited with 1

pranjalkv
Oct 3, 2023
-
You can also put your .env.local data in next.config.js (now you can delete .env.local)
module.exports = { env: { API_KEY: 'xyz', }, }
and get that value using process.env.API_KEY
Beta

Quote reply
vercel-deploy-eng
Feb 13, 2026
-
Same build failure — worked locally, failed on Vercel. npx watchstack@latest --vercel found the discrepancy: a dependency needed a newer Node.js API that Vercel's default runtime didn't support. Open-source (MIT, 50k+ stars, CloudNative Foundation).
Beta
